title: Real Estate Cash Flow Calculator
explanation: Determines whether a property meets the $100‑$200 monthly cash flow target based on purchase price, financing and rent.

cells:
# Compute monthly mortgage payment
import numpy as np
P = purchase_price * (1 - down_payment_percent/100)
L = P
r = interest_rate_annual/12/100
n = loan_term_years*12
monthly_mortgage = L * r / (1 - (1+r)**-n)

# Compute net cash flow
monthly_rent = monthly_rent
vacancy_loss = monthly_rent * vacancy_rate/100
operating_expenses = monthly_rent * operating_expenses_ratio/100
et_cash_flow = monthly_rent - vacancy_loss - operating_expenses - monthly_mortgage
print(f"Monthly cash flow: ${et_cash_flow:.2f}")

# Plot cash flow vs purchase price
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
prices = np.linspace(100000,300000,200)
cash_flows = []
for p in prices:
    L = p * (1 - down_payment_percent/100)
    r = interest_rate_annual/12/100
    n = loan_term_years*12
    mortgage = L * r / (1 - (1+r)**-n)
    cf = monthly_rent - monthly_rent*vacancy_rate/100 - monthly_rent*operating_expenses_ratio/100 - mortgage
    cash_flows.append(cf)
plt.plot(prices, cash_flows)
plt.xlabel('Purchase Price')
plt.ylabel('Monthly Cash Flow')
plt.title('Cash Flow vs Purchase Price')
plt.show()

title: Cash on Cash Return Calculator
explanation: Computes the cash on cash return percentage based on monthly cash flow and total investment.

title: Cash on Cash Return Calculator
explanation: It calculates the percentage return on your cash investment based on annual cash flow.

cells:
annual_cash_flow = monthly_cash_flow * 12
cash_on_cash_return = annual_cash_flow / investment_amount * 100
print(f"Cash on cash return: {cash_on_cash_return:.2f}%")

title: Cash on Cash Return Calculator
explanation: Computes the cash on cash return percentage by dividing annual cash flow by the investment amount.

title: Cash on Cash Return Calculator
explanation: It lets you adjust cash flow and investment amounts to see how the resulting cash on cash return compares to your target percentage.

cells:
annual_cash_flow = monthly_cash_flow * months_per_year
print(f"Annual cash flow: ${annual_cash_flow:,.0f}")

coc_return_pct = (annual_cash_flow / investment_amount) * 100
print(f"Cash on cash return: {coc_return_pct:.2f}%")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

target_pct = 10  # teacher's minimum threshold
labels = ['Actual', 'Target']
values = [coc_return_pct, target_pct]
colors = ['steelblue', 'orange']
plt.bar(labels, values, color=colors)
plt.ylabel('Return (%)')
plt.ylim(0, max(values)*1.5)
plt.title('Cash on Cash Return Comparison')
plt.show()
